data.title: Bossard Holding AG: Stabilising Growth and Balanced Risk/Reward Support a Hold Recommendation
data.description:
Jefferies analyst Fabian Piasta maintained a Hold rating on Bossard Holding AG today and set a price target of CHF165.00.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Fabian Piasta has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors that point to a stabilising but not yet compelling growth profile for Bossard Holding AG. The company’s preliminary fourth-quarter 2025 figures show a solid year-end, with sales rising in the high single digits year-on-year and slightly surpassing market expectations, mainly thanks to a rebound in the US against relatively easy comparables and strength in specific verticals such as energy, rail, and medical. However, European activity remains mixed, with headline revenue supported by currency and price effects while underlying organic growth is flat, as many industrial end-markets continue to be constrained by tariff-related uncertainty. Asia presents a similarly nuanced picture, where near-shoring demand in Malaysia tied to the semiconductor supply chain is offset by weaker trends in China and India, also affected by tariff issues.
Overall full-year 2025 revenues slightly exceeded both the company’s target range and consensus, and management reaffirmed its earnings guidance, which matches market expectations rather than beating them. This combination of modest organic growth, ongoing regional and sectoral headwinds, and an earnings outlook that aligns with rather than outperforms consensus supports a neutral stance. In Piasta’s view, the upcoming guidance for 2026 and potential improvement in European leading indicators, such as purchasing managers’ indices moving sustainably into expansion territory, will be critical to reassessing the upside potential. Until those catalysts provide clearer evidence of a stronger cyclical upturn, the risk/reward appears balanced, justifying a Hold recommendation.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Equinor: Muted Earnings Outlook and Conservative Capital Returns Support Neutral Hold Stance
data.description:
Jason Gabelman, an analyst from TD Cowen, maintained the Hold rating on Equinor ASA. The associated price target remains the same with $22.00.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Jason Gabelman has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors relating to Equinor’s earnings outlook, capital returns, and risk profile. He trims his 4Q25 EPS forecast to align with consensus, reflecting weaker commodity prices, lower-than-expected realizations, and a higher guided tax rate, only partly offset by a one-off operating benefit in Norway and stronger divestment proceeds. While asset sales exceeded prior expectations by about $1 billion, this appears tied to specific transactions and does not fundamentally alter the medium-term earnings trajectory. Overall, the revisions suggest a more muted earnings and free cash flow profile than previously assumed, but not weak enough to warrant a negative rating.
Gabelman also expects the company to announce a 2026 share repurchase program of roughly $2 billion, which is below market expectations and implies a more conservative distribution strategy in the near term. Although projected 2026 distribution and free cash flow yields are still reasonably attractive, they are weighed down by lower commodity prices, lagged Norwegian tax payments, and elevated capex, including substantial spending on the Empire Wind project before related tax benefits are realized. Net debt is projected to rise meaningfully by the end of 2026, adding to balance sheet considerations. In addition, Equinor’s sizeable exposure to global gas markets—where prices are expected to soften—and uncertainty around the strategic direction of its ORSTED investment further temper the risk/reward, leading Gabelman to maintain a neutral, Hold stance rather than recommend buying the stock.

According to TipRanks, Gabelman is a 4-star analyst with an average return of 5.9% and a 57.02% success rate. Gabelman covers the Energy sector, focusing on stocks such as Chevron, Exxon Mobil, and Phillips 66.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
